Title: How to add a dependent to insurance — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

A Engineering employee recently had a baby/got married and needs to add a dependent to their insurance. What is the process?

## Answer

Adding a dependent is a qualifying life event that allows mid-year benefits changes.

### Qualifying Life Events

- Marriage or domestic partnership
- Birth or adoption of a child
- Gaining legal guardianship
- Loss of other coverage (spouse's job loss, aging off parent's plan)

### Deadline

**You have 30 days from the qualifying event to make changes.** After 30 days, you must wait until the next open enrollment.

### Steps to Add a Dependent

1. Log in to the Benefits Portal at benefits.company.com
2. Click "Life Event" from the dashboard
3. Select the type of event (marriage, birth, adoption, etc.)
4. Enter the event date
5. Add dependent information:
   - Full legal name, date of birth, SSN, gender
   - Relationship to employee
6. Select which benefits to enroll the dependent in:
   - Medical, dental, vision
   - Life insurance (if applicable)
7. Upload required documentation:
   - Marriage certificate (for spouse)
   - Birth certificate (for newborn)
   - Adoption decree (for adopted child)
   - Court order (for legal guardianship)
8. Review premium changes and confirm

### Premium Impact

- Adding a dependent changes your coverage tier:
  - Employee Only → Employee + Spouse or Employee + Child(ren) or Family
- New premiums effective the 1st of the month following the event
- Premiums are deducted pre-tax from your paycheck

### Important Notes

- Newborns are covered from birth if added within 30 days
- Stepchildren are eligible upon marriage to the child's parent
- Domestic partners may be added (requires Domestic Partner Affidavit)
- Children covered until age 26 (regardless of student, marital, or employment status)
Author: HR Generalist

Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: COBRA continuation coverage — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

A Engineering employee is leaving the company. How does COBRA continuation coverage work?

## Answer

COBRA allows former employees and their dependents to continue group health coverage at their own expense.

### Eligibility

- Voluntary or involuntary termination (except gross misconduct)
- Reduction in hours making employee ineligible for benefits
- Divorce or legal separation
- Dependent child aging out of coverage
- Employee death (coverage for dependents)

### Timeline

1. **Day 0**: Qualifying event occurs (e.g., last day of employment)
2. **Within 14 days**: Company notifies the COBRA administrator
3. **Within 44 days of event**: COBRA election notice mailed to your home
4. **60 days from notice**: You have 60 days to elect COBRA coverage
5. **45 days after election**: First premium payment due (retroactive to coverage start)

### Coverage Details

- Same medical, dental, and vision plans you had as an employee
- Coverage is retroactive to the day after your group coverage ended
- Duration: up to 18 months (36 months for certain qualifying events)

### Cost

- **You pay 102% of the full premium** (employee share + employer share + 2% admin fee)
- Example: If your medical premium was $250/month as employee, but the full cost was $800/month, you pay $816/month under COBRA
- Premiums must be paid monthly by the 1st of the month (30-day grace period)

### How to Enroll

1. Complete and return the COBRA Election Form within 60 days
2. Select which coverages to continue (medical, dental, vision — independently)
3. Mail payment to the COBRA administrator
4. Coverage is confirmed retroactively

### Alternatives to COBRA

- ACA Marketplace plans (healthcare.gov) — may be more affordable
- Spouse's employer plan (losing coverage is a qualifying event)
- Medicaid (if income-eligible)
- Short-term health insurance (limited coverage)

### Contact

- COBRA Administrator: 800-555-0567
- HR Benefits Team: benefits@company.com
Author: HR Compliance Team

- Example: If your medical premium was $250/month as employee, but the full cost was $800/month, you pay $816/month under COBRA
- Premiums must be paid monthly by the 1st of the month (30-day grace period)

### How to Enroll

1. Complete and return the COBRA Election Form within 60 days
2. Select which coverages to continue (medical, dental, vision — independently)
3. Mail payment to the COBRA administrator
4. Coverage is confirmed retroactively

### Alternatives to COBRA

- ACA Marketplace plans (healthcare.gov) — may be more affordable
- Spouse's employer plan (losing coverage is a qualifying event)
- Medicaid (if income-eligible)
- Short-term health insurance (limited coverage)

### Contact

- COBRA Administrator: 800-555-0567
- HR Benefits Team: benefits@company.com

Title: Commuter benefits program — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How do commuter benefits work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The commuter benefits program allows you to pay for transit and parking with pre-tax dollars.

### Program Details

| Benefit | Monthly Maximum | Tax Savings |
|---------|----------------|-------------|
| Transit (bus, train, vanpool, ferry) | $315/month | Pre-tax (reduces taxable income) |
| Qualified Parking | $315/month | Pre-tax (reduces taxable income) |
| Bike-to-Work | $50/month reimbursement | Post-tax reimbursement |

### How to Enroll

1. Log in to ess.company.com > Benefits > Commuter Benefits
2. Select "Transit", "Parking", or both
3. Set your monthly election amount
4. Choose your delivery method:
   - **Transit**: Loaded onto a commuter benefits debit card
   - **Parking**: Loaded onto a commuter benefits debit card or direct payment to parking provider
5. Changes can be made monthly (effective the following month)

### Using Your Benefits

- **Debit Card**: Use at transit agencies, parking meters, parking garages
- **Mobile App**: Load transit passes onto your phone via the benefits app
- **Reimbursement**: Submit receipts for qualifying expenses if not using the card

### What Qualifies

**Transit**: Public transportation, vanpool (6+ passengers), commuter highway vehicle
**Parking**: Parking at or near your workplace, parking at a location from which you commute via transit

### What Does NOT Qualify

- Gas, tolls, or car maintenance
- Uber/Lyft (rideshare is not qualified transit)
- Parking at home or at a personal appointment
- Bicycle purchase (only maintenance and storage qualify for bike benefit)

### Tax Savings Example

If you spend $200/month on transit:
- Without commuter benefit: $200 comes from after-tax pay (actual cost ~$280 at 30% tax rate)
- With commuter benefit: $200 deducted pre-tax (save ~$80/month = $960/year)
Author: HR Compliance Team

- **Reimbursement**: Submit receipts for qualifying expenses if not using the card

### What Qualifies

**Transit**: Public transportation, vanpool (6+ passengers), commuter highway vehicle
**Parking**: Parking at or near your workplace, parking at a location from which you commute via transit

### What Does NOT Qualify

- Gas, tolls, or car maintenance
- Uber/Lyft (rideshare is not qualified transit)
- Parking at home or at a personal appointment
- Bicycle purchase (only maintenance and storage qualify for bike benefit)

### Tax Savings Example

If you spend $200/month on transit:
- Without commuter benefit: $200 comes from after-tax pay (actual cost ~$280 at 30% tax rate)
- With commuter benefit: $200 deducted pre-tax (save ~$80/month = $960/year)
Author: HR Generalist

Author: Learning & Development

Title: Wellness program and incentives — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What wellness programs and incentives are available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

Our wellness program rewards healthy behaviors with financial incentives.

### Annual Wellness Rewards (up to $500)

| Activity | Reward |
|---------|--------|
| Annual physical / preventive exam | $100 |
| Dental cleaning (each, up to 2) | $25 |
| Biometric screening | $75 |
| Flu shot | $25 |
| Complete health risk assessment | $50 |
| 30-day fitness challenge (per quarter) | $50 |
| Tobacco-free attestation | $100 |
| Mental health check-in (EAP session) | $25 |

### How to Earn Rewards

1. Complete the qualifying activity
2. Log it on the Wellness Portal at wellness.company.com
3. Upload verification (receipt, screenshot, or attestation)
4. Rewards credited to your paycheck within 30 days

### Fitness Programs

- **Gym Membership Subsidy**: Up to $75/month for gym, fitness classes, or fitness apps
  - Submit monthly receipt for reimbursement via the Wellness Portal
  - Qualifying: gym memberships, yoga studios, CrossFit, Peloton, ClassPass, etc.
- **On-Site Fitness Center**: Free access for all employees (Building B, lower level)
  - Hours: 5 AM - 9 PM Monday-Friday, 7 AM - 5 PM weekends
  - Includes cardio, weights, group fitness room, locker rooms with showers
- **Step Challenges**: Quarterly team challenges with prizes
- **Running/Walking Club**: Meets Tuesday and Thursday at lunch

### Mental Wellness

- 12 free EAP counseling sessions per year
- Headspace/Calm app: Free premium subscription for all employees
- Mental Health First Aid training available quarterly
- Wellness rooms available on each floor for quiet breaks

### Tobacco Cessation

- Free cessation program through health plan
- Nicotine replacement therapy covered at 100%
- Coaching and support through EAP
- $100 wellness reward for completing the program
Author: Learning & Development

Title: Pet insurance benefit — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

Does the company offer pet insurance for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Yes! The company offers voluntary pet insurance through Nationwide Pet Insurance at group-discounted rates.

### Coverage Options

| Plan | Monthly Cost (approx.) | Coverage |
|------|----------------------|----------|
| Basic | $20-35 | Accidents only (broken bones, ingestion, lacerations) |
| Standard | $35-55 | Accidents + illnesses (infections, cancer, diabetes) |
| Premium | $55-85 | Accidents + illnesses + wellness (vaccines, dental, checkups) |

*Actual premium depends on pet age, breed, and location*

### What's Covered

**Accidents**: Emergency vet visits, surgery, X-rays, hospitalization, medications
**Illness**: Diagnostics, treatment, prescriptions, specialist visits, chronic conditions
**Wellness (Premium only)**: Annual exam, vaccinations, flea/tick prevention, dental cleaning, spay/neuter

### Eligible Pets

- Dogs and cats of any age or breed
- Birds, reptiles, and exotic pets (separate plan)
- No exclusions for pre-existing conditions on wellness items
- Pre-existing conditions excluded from accident/illness coverage

### Enrollment

1. Visit petinsurance.company.com
2. Enter your pet's information (name, species, breed, age)
3. Select your plan level
4. Choose your deductible ($100, $250, or $500 annual)
5. Choose reimbursement level (70%, 80%, or 90%)
6. Premium is deducted from your paycheck (post-tax)

### How Claims Work

1. Visit any licensed veterinarian (no network restrictions)
2. Pay the vet bill
3. Submit the claim online or via the Nationwide app
4. Reimbursement deposited to your bank within 5-10 business days

### Group Discount

- Company employees receive a 10% group discount
- Multi-pet discount: 5% off for each additional pet
- No waiting period for accidents (15-day waiting period for illness)
Author: Learning & Development

2. Enter your pet's information (name, species, breed, age)

4. Choose your deductible ($100, $250, or $500 annual)

6. Premium is deducted from your paycheck (post-tax)

### How Claims Work

1. Visit any licensed veterinarian (no network restrictions)

3. Submit the claim online or via the Nationwide app
4. Reimbursement deposited to your bank within 5-10 business days

- Company employees receive a 10% group discount
- Multi-pet discount: 5% off for each additional pet
- No waiting period for accidents (15-day waiting period for illness)

Title: Sabbatical leave program — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

Is there a sabbatical program available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company offers paid sabbatical leave as a reward for long service.

### Eligibility

- Full-time employees with 7+ years of continuous service
- Must have satisfactory or above performance rating
- Only one sabbatical per 7-year period

### Sabbatical Details

| Years of Service | Sabbatical Duration | Pay |
|-----------------|-------------------|-----|
| 7-9 years | 4 weeks | 100% base salary |
| 10-14 years | 6 weeks | 100% base salary |
| 15+ years | 8 weeks | 100% base salary |

### How to Request

1. Discuss timing with your manager at least 6 months in advance
2. Submit a Sabbatical Request via the HR Portal
3. Include proposed dates and a brief plan (travel, education, personal project, rest)
4. Manager and HR approve based on business needs
5. Work with your team to create a coverage plan

### During Sabbatical

- All benefits continue as if actively employed
- PTO continues to accrue
- Company equipment may be retained
- No expectation to check email or attend meetings
- Emergency-only contact (life-or-death business situations)

### After Sabbatical

- Return to the same position (or equivalent)
- Welcome-back meeting with manager
- Gradual ramp-up week (lighter workload)
- Share your experience at a team meeting (optional but encouraged)

### Restrictions

- Sabbatical cannot be combined with other leave types
- Cannot be split into multiple periods
- Cannot be taken during critical business periods (determined by manager)
- Unused sabbatical does not accrue or pay out
Author: Payroll Team

1. Discuss timing with your manager at least 6 months in advance
2. Submit a Sabbatical Request via the HR Portal
3. Include proposed dates and a brief plan (travel, education, personal project, rest)
4. Manager and HR approve based on business needs
5. Work with your team to create a coverage plan

### During Sabbatical

- All benefits continue as if actively employed
- PTO continues to accrue
- Company equipment may be retained
- No expectation to check email or attend meetings
- Emergency-only contact (life-or-death business situations)

### After Sabbatical

- Return to the same position (or equivalent)
- Welcome-back meeting with manager
- Gradual ramp-up week (lighter workload)
- Share your experience at a team meeting (optional but encouraged)

### Restrictions

- Sabbatical cannot be combined with other leave types
- Cannot be split into multiple periods
- Cannot be taken during critical business periods (determined by manager)
- Unused sabbatical does not accrue or pay out

Author: HR Operations

Title: Volunteer time off policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

Does the company offer volunteer time off for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Yes! The company encourages community involvement through paid Volunteer Time Off (VTO).

### VTO Allowance

- All full-time employees: **16 hours (2 days) of paid VTO per year**
- Part-time employees: Prorated based on scheduled hours
- VTO is in addition to PTO — it does not reduce your vacation balance

### Eligible Activities

- Volunteering with a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization
- Community service events organized by the company
- Board service for a nonprofit (up to 8 hours/year for board meetings)
- Mentoring programs (Big Brothers/Big Sisters, school mentoring, etc.)
- Disaster relief volunteering
- Pro bono professional services for nonprofits

### Not Eligible

- Political campaigning or fundraising
- Religious proselytizing
- Activities benefiting a for-profit organization
- Personal errands or favors for friends/family

### How to Request VTO

1. Find a volunteer opportunity
2. Request VTO through the Time & Attendance system (select "Volunteer Time Off")
3. Include the organization name and activity description
4. Manager approves based on scheduling needs
5. After volunteering, log your hours and share a brief recap

### Company Volunteer Events

- Quarterly company-sponsored volunteer days (additional to individual VTO)
- Annual Day of Service (company-wide, typically in October)
- Team volunteer outings can be arranged through HR
- Matching donations: Company matches employee charitable donations up to $500/year

### Recognition

- Top volunteers recognized at the annual company meeting
- Volunteer hours tracked and included in performance reviews under Cultural Goals
Author: Payroll Team

Author: HR Operations

Title: How to donate PTO to a coworker — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

Can a Engineering employee donate PTO to a coworker experiencing hardship?

## Answer

Yes, the PTO Donation Program allows employees to donate accrued PTO to colleagues facing extended medical or family hardships.

### Eligibility to Receive Donated PTO

The employee must:
- Have exhausted all of their own PTO, sick leave, and applicable disability benefits
- Be experiencing a qualifying hardship:
  - Own serious medical condition
  - Caring for a seriously ill family member
  - Natural disaster affecting their home
  - Other extraordinary circumstances (approved by HR on case-by-case basis)
- Have their situation approved by HR

### How to Request Donated PTO

1. Employee (or their designee) contacts HR at hr-leave@company.com
2. HR verifies eligibility and the employee's leave balances
3. HR sends an anonymous appeal to employees (if the recipient consents to an appeal)
4. Alternatively, employees can donate directly without an appeal

### How to Donate PTO

1. Log in to the Time & Attendance system
2. Go to "PTO Donation" under the Time Off menu
3. Enter:
   - Recipient's name (if responding to an appeal) or "General PTO Bank"
   - Number of hours to donate (minimum 4 hours, maximum 40 hours per request)
4. Submit — hours are deducted from your PTO balance immediately

### Rules

- Donated PTO is irrevocable (you cannot take it back)
- You must retain at least 40 hours of PTO after donating
- Donated hours are converted at the recipient's pay rate
- PTO bank: Unused donated PTO remains in the bank for future needs
- Tax implications: Donors are not taxed; recipients are taxed on PTO received as regular income

### General PTO Bank

- Employees can donate to the general PTO bank at any time
- The bank is used for approved hardship cases throughout the year
- HR administers distributions from the bank

- Donated PTO is irrevocable (you cannot take it back)
- You must retain at least 40 hours of PTO after donating
- Donated hours are converted at the recipient's pay rate
- PTO bank: Unused donated PTO remains in the bank for future needs
- Tax implications: Donors are not taxed; recipients are taxed on PTO received as regular income

### General PTO Bank

- Employees can donate to the general PTO bank at any time
- The bank is used for approved hardship cases throughout the year
- HR administers distributions from the bank
Author: HR Operations

Title: How to donate PTO to a coworker — Executive department
Content:
## Question

Can a Executive employee donate PTO to a coworker experiencing hardship?

## Answer

Yes, the PTO Donation Program allows employees to donate accrued PTO to colleagues facing extended medical or family hardships.

### Eligibility to Receive Donated PTO

The employee must:
- Have exhausted all of their own PTO, sick leave, and applicable disability benefits
- Be experiencing a qualifying hardship:
  - Own serious medical condition
  - Caring for a seriously ill family member
  - Natural disaster affecting their home
  - Other extraordinary circumstances (approved by HR on case-by-case basis)
- Have their situation approved by HR

### How to Request Donated PTO

1. Employee (or their designee) contacts HR at hr-leave@company.com
2. HR verifies eligibility and the employee's leave balances
3. HR sends an anonymous appeal to employees (if the recipient consents to an appeal)
4. Alternatively, employees can donate directly without an appeal

### How to Donate PTO

1. Log in to the Time & Attendance system
2. Go to "PTO Donation" under the Time Off menu
3. Enter:
   - Recipient's name (if responding to an appeal) or "General PTO Bank"
   - Number of hours to donate (minimum 4 hours, maximum 40 hours per request)
4. Submit — hours are deducted from your PTO balance immediately

### Rules

- Donated PTO is irrevocable (you cannot take it back)
- You must retain at least 40 hours of PTO after donating
- Donated hours are converted at the recipient's pay rate
- PTO bank: Unused donated PTO remains in the bank for future needs
- Tax implications: Donors are not taxed; recipients are taxed on PTO received as regular income

### General PTO Bank

- Employees can donate to the general PTO bank at any time
- The bank is used for approved hardship cases throughout the year
- HR administers distributions from the bank

Title: Military leave and USERRA rights — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What military leave protections are available to Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company fully supports employees who serve in the military, in compliance with the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act (USERRA).

### Military Leave Types

| Leave Type | Duration | Pay |
|-----------|----------|-----|
| Annual training (Reserve/Guard) | Up to 15 days/year | Full pay differential (company pays difference between military and civilian pay) |
| Active duty deployment | Up to 5 years cumulative | Pay differential for first 12 months; unpaid after |
| Weekend drills (Reserve/Guard) | As required | Not applicable (non-work days) |
| Military medical leave | As needed | Per STD/FMLA policies |

### Requesting Military Leave

1. Provide advance notice to your manager (verbal or written) as soon as orders are received
2. Submit a copy of military orders to HR
3. HR processes the leave and benefits continuation
4. Note: Advance notice is not required if military necessity prevents it

### Benefits During Military Leave

- **Health Insurance**: Continues for up to 24 months; you pay employee share for first 30 days, then up to 102% COBRA rate
- **Life Insurance**: Continues for 12 months
- **401(k)**: Contributions resume upon return; company makes up missed matching contributions
- **PTO**: Does not accrue during unpaid military leave

### Reemployment Rights (USERRA)

- **Job Protection**: Right to return to the same position (or equivalent) upon return
- **Seniority**: Treated as if continuously employed for seniority-based benefits
- **Reporting Deadline**:
  - Service < 31 days: Next scheduled work day (after travel + 8 hours rest)
  - Service 31-180 days: Within 14 days
  - Service > 180 days: Within 90 days
- **No Retaliation**: Cannot be terminated without cause for 180 days (1 year if service > 180 days)

### Returning from Deployment

1. Notify HR of your expected return date
2. Provide documentation of completion of service
3. HR coordinates your return and any retraining needed
4. Retraining period at full pay if job has changed during absence

What military leave protections are available to Executive employees?

## Answer

The company fully supports employees who serve in the military, in compliance with the Uniformed Services Employment and Reemployment Rights Act (USERRA).

### Military Leave Types

| Leave Type | Duration | Pay |
|-----------|----------|-----|
| Annual training (Reserve/Guard) | Up to 15 days/year | Full pay differential (company pays difference between military and civilian pay) |
| Active duty deployment | Up to 5 years cumulative | Pay differential for first 12 months; unpaid after |
| Weekend drills (Reserve/Guard) | As required | Not applicable (non-work days) |
| Military medical leave | As needed | Per STD/FMLA policies |

### Requesting Military Leave

1. Provide advance notice to your manager (verbal or written) as soon as orders are received
2. Submit a copy of military orders to HR
3. HR processes the leave and benefits continuation
4. Note: Advance notice is not required if military necessity prevents it

### Benefits During Military Leave

- **Health Insurance**: Continues for up to 24 months; you pay employee share for first 30 days, then up to 102% COBRA rate
- **Life Insurance**: Continues for 12 months
- **401(k)**: Contributions resume upon return; company makes up missed matching contributions
- **PTO**: Does not accrue during unpaid military leave

### Reemployment Rights (USERRA)

- **Job Protection**: Right to return to the same position (or equivalent) upon return
- **Seniority**: Treated as if continuously employed for seniority-based benefits
- **Reporting Deadline**:
  - Service < 31 days: Next scheduled work day (after travel + 8 hours rest)
  - Service 31-180 days: Within 14 days
  - Service > 180 days: Within 90 days
- **No Retaliation**: Cannot be terminated without cause for 180 days (1 year if service > 180 days)

### Returning from Deployment

1. Notify HR of your expected return date
2. Provide documentation of completion of service
3. HR coordinates your return and any retraining needed
4. Retraining period at full pay if job has changed during absence

Title: 90-day probationary period — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What should Engineering employees know about the 90-day probationary period?

## Answer

All new hires go through a 90-day introductory period to ensure mutual fit.

### Purpose

- Evaluate the employee's performance, skills, and cultural fit
- Give the employee time to learn the role and assess the company
- Establish performance expectations and development needs

### What Happens During the 90 Days

**Week 1-2: Orientation Phase**
- Complete onboarding checklist and required training
- Meet team members and key stakeholders
- Review role expectations and 90-day goals with manager
- Set up regular 1:1 meetings (recommended: weekly)

**Day 30: First Check-in**
- Manager completes the 30-Day Review form
- Discussion topics: initial impressions, training progress, questions, early feedback
- Identify any concerns or areas needing support

**Day 60: Mid-Point Review**
- Manager completes the 60-Day Review form
- More substantive performance feedback
- Adjust goals or provide additional resources if needed
- Employee provides feedback on their experience

**Day 90: Final Evaluation**
- Manager completes the 90-Day Review form
- Formal assessment of performance against goals
- Decision: confirm employment, extend probation (up to 30 additional days), or separate
- Employee signs acknowledgment

### Key Policies During Probation

| Policy | During Probation | After Probation |
|--------|-----------------|-----------------|
| PTO usage | Accrues from Day 1, available after 30 days | Fully available |
| Benefits enrollment | Eligible from Day 1, effective 1st of following month | No change |
| Internal transfers | Not eligible | Eligible after 12 months |
| Remote work | Generally not available | Per remote work policy |
| Performance rating | Not included in annual cycle | Included |

### Separation During Probation

- Either party may end the employment relationship during probation
- No formal PIP process required (but feedback should be documented)
- Standard offboarding process applies
- Accrued PTO is paid out
Author: HR Operations

- Decision: confirm employment, extend probation (up to 30 additional days), or separate
- Employee signs acknowledgment

### Key Policies During Probation

| Policy | During Probation | After Probation |
|--------|-----------------|-----------------|
| PTO usage | Accrues from Day 1, available after 30 days | Fully available |
| Benefits enrollment | Eligible from Day 1, effective 1st of following month | No change |
| Internal transfers | Not eligible | Eligible after 12 months |
| Remote work | Generally not available | Per remote work policy |
| Performance rating | Not included in annual cycle | Included |

### Separation During Probation

- Either party may end the employment relationship during probation
- No formal PIP process required (but feedback should be documented)
- Standard offboarding process applies
- Accrued PTO is paid out

Author: Payroll Team

Title: How to order business cards — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does a Engineering employee order business cards?

## Answer

Business cards can be ordered through the company's print portal.

### Standard Business Cards

- Provided to all client-facing employees and managers
- Standard design follows brand guidelines
- 250 cards per order

### Ordering Process

1. Log in to print.company.com with your company SSO
2. Select "Business Cards" from the product menu
3. Verify your information:
   - Full name (as you'd like it printed)
   - Title
   - Department
   - Phone number
   - Email address
   - Office location
4. Preview the card design
5. Submit for manager approval
6. Cards printed and delivered within 5-7 business days

### Delivery

- **Office employees**: Delivered to your desk via interoffice mail
- **Remote employees**: Shipped to your home address (USPS)

### Changes and Reorders

- Name or title change: Submit a new order
- Reorders: Same process, no approval needed for unchanged information
- Rush orders: Available for $25 surcharge (2-business-day turnaround)

### Digital Business Cards

- Available through the company's digital card app
- Share via QR code, NFC tap, email, or text
- Automatically syncs with your company directory information
- Request access through IT (no manager approval needed)

### Cost

- Standard business cards: Covered by the company
- Premium options (special paper, foil, etc.): Requires department budget approval
- Digital business cards: Free
Author: Learning & Development

   - Phone number
   - Email address
   - Office location
4. Preview the card design
5. Submit for manager approval
6. Cards printed and delivered within 5-7 business days

### Delivery

- **Office employees**: Delivered to your desk via interoffice mail
- **Remote employees**: Shipped to your home address (USPS)

### Changes and Reorders

- Name or title change: Submit a new order
- Reorders: Same process, no approval needed for unchanged information
- Rush orders: Available for $25 surcharge (2-business-day turnaround)

### Digital Business Cards

- Available through the company's digital card app
- Share via QR code, NFC tap, email, or text
- Automatically syncs with your company directory information
- Request access through IT (no manager approval needed)

### Cost

- Standard business cards: Covered by the company
- Premium options (special paper, foil, etc.): Requires department budget approval
- Digital business cards: Free
Author: HR Operations

Title: How to order business cards — Administration department
Content:
## Question

How does a Administration employee order business cards?

## Answer

Business cards can be ordered through the company's print portal.

### Standard Business Cards

- Provided to all client-facing employees and managers
- Standard design follows brand guidelines
- 250 cards per order

### Ordering Process

1. Log in to print.company.com with your company SSO
2. Select "Business Cards" from the product menu
3. Verify your information:
   - Full name (as you'd like it printed)

   - Department

- **Remote employees**: Shipped to your home address (USPS)

- Rush orders: Available for $25 surcharge (2-business-day turnaround)

### Digital Business Cards

- Standard business cards: Covered by the company
- Premium options (special paper, foil, etc.): Requires department budget approval
- Digital business cards: Free
Author: Payroll Team

Title: Retiring from the company — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What does a Engineering employee need to know about retiring from the company?

## Answer

Congratulations on your upcoming retirement! Here's what you need to know.

### Notification

- Notify your manager and HR at least 60 days before your planned retirement date
- Submit a formal retirement letter (HR Portal > Forms > Retirement Notice)
- This allows time for knowledge transfer, benefits coordination, and celebration

### Retirement Benefits

**Retiree Health Insurance**
- Employees retiring at age 55+ with 10+ years of service are eligible
- Company subsidizes 50% of premium until Medicare eligibility (age 65)
- After 65: Company-sponsored Medicare supplement plan available
- Dental and vision: Available at full premium (no company subsidy)

**401(k) and Pension**
- 401(k): Remains in the plan or can be rolled to an IRA
- Required minimum distributions begin at age 73
- Company pension (if vested): Contact benefits@company.com for pension estimate
- Schedule a meeting with the retirement plan advisor (free)

**Life Insurance**
- Basic life insurance: Reduced to $10,000 (company-paid) for retirees
- Voluntary life: Can be converted to an individual policy within 31 days of retirement

**Other Benefits**
- Employee discount program: Access continues for 2 years post-retirement
- Company events: Retirees invited to annual holiday party and summer picnic
- Alumni network: Join the company retirees group for networking and social events

### Retirement Process Timeline

| Timing | Action |
|--------|--------|
| 60 days before | Submit retirement notice |
| 45 days before | Benefits transition meeting with HR |
| 30 days before | Begin knowledge transfer |
| 2 weeks before | Return company property |
| Last week | Farewell activities, exit interview |
| Last day | Final paycheck includes PTO payout |
| 30 days after | COBRA notice mailed (if opting out of retiree plan) |

### Phased Retirement

- Available for employees age 60+ with 15+ years of service
- Reduce to 50-80% schedule for up to 12 months before full retirement
- Benefits continue at full-time rates
- Must be approved by manager and HR VP
Author: HR Generalist

- Company pension (if vested): Contact benefits@company.com for pension estimate
- Schedule a meeting with the retirement plan advisor (free)

**Life Insurance**
- Basic life insurance: Reduced to $10,000 (company-paid) for retirees
- Voluntary life: Can be converted to an individual policy within 31 days of retirement

**Other Benefits**
- Employee discount program: Access continues for 2 years post-retirement
- Company events: Retirees invited to annual holiday party and summer picnic
- Alumni network: Join the company retirees group for networking and social events

### Retirement Process Timeline

| Timing | Action |
|--------|--------|
| 60 days before | Submit retirement notice |
| 45 days before | Benefits transition meeting with HR |
| 30 days before | Begin knowledge transfer |
| 2 weeks before | Return company property |
| Last week | Farewell activities, exit interview |
| Last day | Final paycheck includes PTO payout |
| 30 days after | COBRA notice mailed (if opting out of retiree plan) |

### Phased Retirement

- Available for employees age 60+ with 15+ years of service
- Reduce to 50-80% schedule for up to 12 months before full retirement
- Benefits continue at full-time rates
- Must be approved by manager and HR VP
Author: Talent Acquisition

Title: Retiring from the company — Administration department
Content:
## Question

What does a Administration employee need to know about retiring from the company?

## Answer

Congratulations on your upcoming retirement! Here's what you need to know.

### Notification

- Notify your manager and HR at least 60 days before your planned retirement date
- Submit a formal retirement letter (HR Portal > Forms > Retirement Notice)
- This allows time for knowledge transfer, benefits coordination, and celebration

### Retirement Benefits

**Retiree Health Insurance**
- Employees retiring at age 55+ with 10+ years of service are eligible
- Company subsidizes 50% of premium until Medicare eligibility (age 65)
- After 65: Company-sponsored Medicare supplement plan available
- Dental and vision: Available at full premium (no company subsidy)

**401(k) and Pension**
- 401(k): Remains in the plan or can be rolled to an IRA
- Required minimum distributions begin at age 73
- Company pension (if vested): Contact benefits@company.com for pension estimate
- Schedule a meeting with the retirement plan advisor (free)

**Life Insurance**
- Basic life insurance: Reduced to $10,000 (company-paid) for retirees
- Voluntary life: Can be converted to an individual policy within 31 days of retirement

**Other Benefits**
- Employee discount program: Access continues for 2 years post-retirement
- Company events: Retirees invited to annual holiday party and summer picnic
- Alumni network: Join the company retirees group for networking and social events

### Retirement Process Timeline

| Timing | Action |
|--------|--------|
| 60 days before | Submit retirement notice |
| 45 days before | Benefits transition meeting with HR |
| 30 days before | Begin knowledge transfer |
| 2 weeks before | Return company property |
| Last week | Farewell activities, exit interview |
| Last day | Final paycheck includes PTO payout |
| 30 days after | COBRA notice mailed (if opting out of retiree plan) |

### Phased Retirement

- Available for employees age 60+ with 15+ years of service
- Reduce to 50-80% schedule for up to 12 months before full retirement
- Benefits continue at full-time rates
- Must be approved by manager and HR VP
Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: 360-degree feedback process — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does the 360-degree feedback process work in Engineering?

## Answer

360-degree feedback collects input from multiple perspectives to provide a comprehensive view of an employee's performance and behaviors.

### Who Participates

| Rater Type | Number | Required? |
|-----------|--------|-----------|
| Self | 1 | Yes |
| Manager | 1 | Yes |
| Direct Reports | All (if applicable) | Yes |
| Peers | 3-5 (employee selects, manager approves) | Yes |
| External Stakeholders | 1-2 (optional) | No |

### Timeline

| Phase | Dates | Activity |
|-------|-------|----------|
| Rater Selection | October 15-31 | Employee selects peers, manager approves |
| Survey Distribution | November 1 | Surveys sent to all raters |
| Survey Completion | November 1-14 | Raters complete anonymous surveys |
| Report Generation | November 15-20 | HR generates 360 reports |
| Feedback Delivery | November 20 - December 15 | Manager reviews report with employee |

### Survey Content

The 360 survey evaluates competencies such as:
- **Communication**: Clarity, listening, written and verbal skills
- **Collaboration**: Teamwork, cross-functional partnership, conflict resolution
- **Leadership**: Vision, coaching, decision-making, accountability (for managers)
- **Technical Excellence**: Job knowledge, quality of work, innovation
- **Customer Focus**: Responsiveness, service orientation, relationship building
- **Values Alignment**: Integrity, respect, inclusion, growth mindset

Each competency is rated on a 5-point scale with open-ended comments.

### Confidentiality

- Individual peer and direct report responses are anonymous
- Comments are aggregated (minimum 3 responses to show category results)
- Manager feedback is identified (not anonymous)
- Self-assessment is shared with manager only

### Using the Feedback

- Identify strengths to leverage and blind spots to address
- Create a development plan based on themes
- 360 results inform (but do not determine) the performance rating
- Focus on patterns, not individual comments

- Comments are aggregated (minimum 3 responses to show category results)
- Manager feedback is identified (not anonymous)
- Self-assessment is shared with manager only

### Using the Feedback

- Identify strengths to leverage and blind spots to address
- Create a development plan based on themes
- 360 results inform (but do not determine) the performance rating
- Focus on patterns, not individual comments
Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: 360-degree feedback process — Administration department
Content:
## Question

How does the 360-degree feedback process work in Administration?

## Answer

360-degree feedback collects input from multiple perspectives to provide a comprehensive view of an employee's performance and behaviors.

### Who Participates

| Rater Type | Number | Required? |
|-----------|--------|-----------|
| Self | 1 | Yes |
| Manager | 1 | Yes |
| Direct Reports | All (if applicable) | Yes |
| Peers | 3-5 (employee selects, manager approves) | Yes |
| External Stakeholders | 1-2 (optional) | No |

### Timeline

| Phase | Dates | Activity |
|-------|-------|----------|
| Rater Selection | October 15-31 | Employee selects peers, manager approves |
| Survey Distribution | November 1 | Surveys sent to all raters |
| Survey Completion | November 1-14 | Raters complete anonymous surveys |
| Report Generation | November 15-20 | HR generates 360 reports |
| Feedback Delivery | November 20 - December 15 | Manager reviews report with employee |

### Survey Content

The 360 survey evaluates competencies such as:
- **Communication**: Clarity, listening, written and verbal skills
- **Collaboration**: Teamwork, cross-functional partnership, conflict resolution
- **Leadership**: Vision, coaching, decision-making, accountability (for managers)
- **Technical Excellence**: Job knowledge, quality of work, innovation
- **Customer Focus**: Responsiveness, service orientation, relationship building
- **Values Alignment**: Integrity, respect, inclusion, growth mindset

Each competency is rated on a 5-point scale with open-ended comments.

### Confidentiality

- Individual peer and direct report responses are anonymous
- Comments are aggregated (minimum 3 responses to show category results)
- Manager feedback is identified (not anonymous)
- Self-assessment is shared with manager only

### Using the Feedback

- Identify strengths to leverage and blind spots to address
- Create a development plan based on themes
- 360 results inform (but do not determine) the performance rating
- Focus on patterns, not individual comments
Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Career pathing and job architecture — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does the career pathing framework work in Engineering?

## Answer

The company uses a structured job architecture to provide clear career progression paths.

### Job Level Framework

| Level | Title Examples | Scope |
|-------|--------------|-------|
| L1 | Associate, Coordinator | Entry-level, learning role fundamentals |
| L2 | Specialist, Analyst | Independent contributor, deepening expertise |
| L3 | Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst | Advanced individual contributor, mentors others |
| L4 | Lead, Principal | Technical expert or small team lead |
| L5 | Manager | People manager, owns team outcomes |
| L6 | Senior Manager, Director | Multi-team or function leader |
| L7 | VP | Department or business unit leader |
| L8 | SVP, C-Level | Executive leadership |

### Dual Career Track

The company offers parallel tracks for individual contributors (IC) and people managers:

**Individual Contributor Track**: L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 (Lead/Principal) → L4+ (Distinguished/Fellow)
**Management Track**: L1 → L2 → L3 → L5 (Manager) → L6 → L7 → L8

- Moving from IC to management (or vice versa) is supported
- IC and management tracks are equally valued and compensated at equivalent levels
- Not everyone needs to become a manager to advance

### Career Development Resources

1. **Job Architecture Guide**: Detailed competency requirements for each level
   - Available on HR Portal > Career > Job Architecture
   - Includes skills matrix, behavioral expectations, and scope of impact

2. **Career Conversations**: Semi-annual discussion with your manager
   - Review current level and next-level requirements
   - Identify gaps and create a development plan
   - Explore lateral moves or cross-functional opportunities

3. **Internal Mobility**: Browse open roles at jobs.company.com
   - Shadow programs available (spend 1-2 days observing another role)
   - Rotation programs for high-potential employees (6-month rotations)

4. **Skills Assessment**: Self-assessment tool on the LMS
   - Maps your skills to various career paths
   - Suggests learning resources for gaps

### Compensation by Level

- Each level has a defined pay band (min, mid, max)
- Pay bands are benchmarked annually against market data
- Your position in the band depends on experience, performance, and tenure
- Pay band details available from your manager or HR
Author: Employee Relations Team

| L2 | Specialist, Analyst | Independent contributor, deepening expertise |
| L3 | Senior Specialist, Senior Analyst | Advanced individual contributor, mentors others |
| L4 | Lead, Principal | Technical expert or small team lead |
| L5 | Manager | People manager, owns team outcomes |
| L6 | Senior Manager, Director | Multi-team or function leader |
| L7 | VP | Department or business unit leader |
| L8 | SVP, C-Level | Executive leadership |

### Dual Career Track

The company offers parallel tracks for individual contributors (IC) and people managers:

**Individual Contributor Track**: L1 → L2 → L3 → L4 (Lead/Principal) → L4+ (Distinguished/Fellow)
**Management Track**: L1 → L2 → L3 → L5 (Manager) → L6 → L7 → L8

- Moving from IC to management (or vice versa) is supported
- IC and management tracks are equally valued and compensated at equivalent levels
- Not everyone needs to become a manager to advance

### Career Development Resources

1. **Job Architecture Guide**: Detailed competency requirements for each level
   - Available on HR Portal > Career > Job Architecture
   - Includes skills matrix, behavioral expectations, and scope of impact

2. **Career Conversations**: Semi-annual discussion with your manager
   - Review current level and next-level requirements
   - Identify gaps and create a development plan
   - Explore lateral moves or cross-functional opportunities

3. **Internal Mobility**: Browse open roles at jobs.company.com
   - Shadow programs available (spend 1-2 days observing another role)
   - Rotation programs for high-potential employees (6-month rotations)

4. **Skills Assessment**: Self-assessment tool on the LMS
   - Maps your skills to various career paths
   - Suggests learning resources for gaps

### Compensation by Level

- Each level has a defined pay band (min, mid, max)
- Pay bands are benchmarked annually against market data
- Your position in the band depends on experience, performance, and tenure
- Pay band details available from your manager or HR

Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: Religious accommodation requests — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does a Engineering employee request a religious accommodation?

## Answer

The company respects employees' religious beliefs and practices and provides reasonable accommodations.

### Types of Religious Accommodations

- **Schedule Modifications**: Adjusted start/end times for prayer, worship services, or religious observances
- **Religious Holidays**: Time off for holidays not on the company calendar (use floating holidays, PTO, or unpaid leave)
- **Dress and Grooming**: Exceptions to dress code for religious attire (hijab, turban, yarmulke, etc.)
- **Dietary Needs**: Accommodation during company events or cafeteria options
- **Prayer Space**: Access to a quiet room for prayer during the workday
- **Work Duties**: Modification of duties that conflict with sincerely held beliefs

### Request Process

1. Notify your manager or HR of the need for accommodation
2. Submit an Accommodation Request form via the HR Portal (optional but recommended for documentation)
3. HR engages in an interactive process:
   - Discuss the specific religious practice and need
   - Explore accommodation options
   - Consider impact on operations and coworkers
4. HR provides a decision within 10 business days
5. If approved, accommodation is documented and implemented

### What the Company May Ask

- The nature of the religious belief or practice
- How the accommodation would help
- The company may NOT:
  - Question the validity or sincerity of your beliefs
  - Require you to belong to a recognized religion
  - Treat you differently because of your request

### Available Resources

- Prayer/meditation rooms: Available on each floor (Room 110, 210, 310)
- Floating holidays: 2 per year, can be used for any religious observance
- Interfaith employee resource group: interfaith@company.com

### Undue Hardship

- The company may deny a request if it creates undue hardship (more than minimal cost or disruption)
- If denied, HR will work with you to find alternative accommodations
- You may appeal the decision to the VP of HR within 30 days
Author: Payroll Team

- **Prayer Space**: Access to a quiet room for prayer during the workday
- **Work Duties**: Modification of duties that conflict with sincerely held beliefs

### Request Process

1. Notify your manager or HR of the need for accommodation
2. Submit an Accommodation Request form via the HR Portal (optional but recommended for documentation)
3. HR engages in an interactive process:
   - Discuss the specific religious practice and need
   - Explore accommodation options
   - Consider impact on operations and coworkers
4. HR provides a decision within 10 business days
5. If approved, accommodation is documented and implemented

### What the Company May Ask

- The nature of the religious belief or practice
- How the accommodation would help
- The company may NOT:
  - Question the validity or sincerity of your beliefs
  - Require you to belong to a recognized religion
  - Treat you differently because of your request

### Available Resources

- Prayer/meditation rooms: Available on each floor (Room 110, 210, 310)
- Floating holidays: 2 per year, can be used for any religious observance
- Interfaith employee resource group: interfaith@company.com

### Undue Hardship

- The company may deny a request if it creates undue hardship (more than minimal cost or disruption)
- If denied, HR will work with you to find alternative accommodations
- You may appeal the decision to the VP of HR within 30 days

Author: HR Operations

Title: Workplace romance and fraternization policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What is the company policy on workplace relationships for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company recognizes that workplace relationships may develop and has guidelines to protect all parties.

### Policy Overview

**Permitted Relationships (with disclosure):**
- Relationships between employees in different departments with no reporting relationship
- Relationships between employees at the same level in the same department

**Requires Immediate Disclosure:**
- Any romantic or sexual relationship between employees
- Disclosure is made to HR (not broadcast to the team)

**Prohibited Relationships:**
- Between a manager and their direct report (at any level in the chain)
- Between an HR employee and anyone they support
- Between an employee and someone they evaluate, influence compensation for, or make hiring/firing decisions about

### Disclosure Process

1. Both parties complete the Relationship Disclosure Form (confidential, submitted to HR)
2. HR reviews for conflicts of interest
3. If a conflict exists, HR works with both parties and their managers to resolve it:
   - Transfer of one party to a different team
   - Change in reporting structure
   - Other arrangements to eliminate the conflict
4. Both parties sign a Consensual Relationship Agreement acknowledging:
   - The relationship is voluntary
   - They understand the harassment policy
   - They will report any changes in the relationship status

### If a Relationship Ends

- Notify HR if the relationship ends
- Both parties are expected to maintain professional conduct
- HR monitors for any retaliatory or harassing behavior
- EAP is available for support

### Consequences of Non-Disclosure

- Failure to disclose a prohibited relationship may result in disciplinary action up to termination
- The person in the position of authority bears greater responsibility for disclosure
- Even disclosed prohibited relationships require structural changes

### Important Notes

- This policy applies to all employees, contractors, interns, and temporary workers
- HR treats all disclosures confidentially (shared only on a need-to-know basis)
- No employee will face retaliation for making a good-faith disclosure

- Between an employee and someone they evaluate, influence compensation for, or make hiring/firing decisions about

### Disclosure Process

1. Both parties complete the Relationship Disclosure Form (confidential, submitted to HR)
2. HR reviews for conflicts of interest
3. If a conflict exists, HR works with both parties and their managers to resolve it:
   - Transfer of one party to a different team
   - Change in reporting structure
   - Other arrangements to eliminate the conflict
4. Both parties sign a Consensual Relationship Agreement acknowledging:
   - The relationship is voluntary
   - They understand the harassment policy
   - They will report any changes in the relationship status

### If a Relationship Ends

- Notify HR if the relationship ends
- Both parties are expected to maintain professional conduct
- HR monitors for any retaliatory or harassing behavior
- EAP is available for support

### Consequences of Non-Disclosure

- Failure to disclose a prohibited relationship may result in disciplinary action up to termination
- The person in the position of authority bears greater responsibility for disclosure
- Even disclosed prohibited relationships require structural changes

### Important Notes

- This policy applies to all employees, contractors, interns, and temporary workers
- HR treats all disclosures confidentially (shared only on a need-to-know basis)
- No employee will face retaliation for making a good-faith disclosure
Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Workplace romance and fraternization policy — Administration department
Content:
## Question

What is the company policy on workplace relationships for Administration employees?

## Answer

The company recognizes that workplace relationships may develop and has guidelines to protect all parties.

### Policy Overview

**Permitted Relationships (with disclosure):**
- Relationships between employees in different departments with no reporting relationship
- Relationships between employees at the same level in the same department

**Requires Immediate Disclosure:**
- Any romantic or sexual relationship between employees
- Disclosure is made to HR (not broadcast to the team)

**Prohibited Relationships:**
- Between a manager and their direct report (at any level in the chain)
- Between an HR employee and anyone they support
- Between an employee and someone they evaluate, influence compensation for, or make hiring/firing decisions about

### Disclosure Process

1. Both parties complete the Relationship Disclosure Form (confidential, submitted to HR)
2. HR reviews for conflicts of interest
3. If a conflict exists, HR works with both parties and their managers to resolve it:
   - Transfer of one party to a different team
   - Change in reporting structure
   - Other arrangements to eliminate the conflict
4. Both parties sign a Consensual Relationship Agreement acknowledging:
   - The relationship is voluntary
   - They understand the harassment policy
   - They will report any changes in the relationship status

### If a Relationship Ends

- Notify HR if the relationship ends
- Both parties are expected to maintain professional conduct
- HR monitors for any retaliatory or harassing behavior
- EAP is available for support

### Consequences of Non-Disclosure

- Failure to disclose a prohibited relationship may result in disciplinary action up to termination
- The person in the position of authority bears greater responsibility for disclosure
- Even disclosed prohibited relationships require structural changes

### Important Notes

- This policy applies to all employees, contractors, interns, and temporary workers
- HR treats all disclosures confidentially (shared only on a need-to-know basis)
- No employee will face retaliation for making a good-faith disclosure
Author: HR Generalist

Title: Dress code and appearance policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What is the dress code policy for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company maintains a business casual dress code with flexibility for different work environments.

### Dress Code by Setting

**Office (Business Casual)**
- Collared shirts, blouses, sweaters, cardigans
- Slacks, chinos, skirts, dresses
- Clean, closed-toe shoes (sneakers acceptable if clean and professional)
- Jeans allowed on Fridays and during designated casual periods

**Client-Facing / External Meetings (Business Professional)**
- Suits, blazers, professional dresses
- Dress shoes
- Conservative accessories
- Your manager will advise when business professional is expected

**Casual Fridays / Company Events**
- Jeans, polo shirts, company-branded apparel
- Sneakers, sandals (with good judgment)

**Remote Work / Video Calls**
- Business casual from the waist up (at minimum) for video calls
- Use good judgment based on who you're meeting with

### Always Acceptable

- Religious or cultural attire (hijab, turban, yarmulke, etc.)
- Attire required for medical reasons
- Company-branded clothing
- Smart casual: neat and professional in appearance

### Not Acceptable

- Clothing with offensive graphics, slogans, or language
- Ripped, torn, or excessively worn clothing
- Athletic wear (gym clothes, sweatpants) — unless in the fitness center
- Revealing or excessively tight clothing
- Strong fragrances or colognes
- Flip-flops (safety concern in office environments)

### Tattoos and Piercings

- Tattoos are generally acceptable
- Offensive tattoos must be covered
- Piercings are generally acceptable
- Use good judgment for client-facing situations

### Accommodations

- Religious, medical, or disability-related dress accommodations: Contact HR
- Temperature-related accommodations: Layers are encouraged; contact Facilities for workspace adjustments

### Enforcement

- Managers address dress code concerns privately and respectfully
- First occurrence: Verbal reminder
- Repeated issues: Written counseling
- Focus is on maintaining a professional, inclusive environment
Author: Learning & Development

- Sneakers, sandals (with good judgment)

**Remote Work / Video Calls**
- Business casual from the waist up (at minimum) for video calls
- Use good judgment based on who you're meeting with

### Always Acceptable

- Religious or cultural attire (hijab, turban, yarmulke, etc.)
- Attire required for medical reasons
- Company-branded clothing
- Smart casual: neat and professional in appearance

### Not Acceptable

- Clothing with offensive graphics, slogans, or language
- Ripped, torn, or excessively worn clothing
- Athletic wear (gym clothes, sweatpants) — unless in the fitness center
- Revealing or excessively tight clothing
- Strong fragrances or colognes
- Flip-flops (safety concern in office environments)

### Tattoos and Piercings

- Tattoos are generally acceptable
- Offensive tattoos must be covered
- Piercings are generally acceptable
- Use good judgment for client-facing situations

### Accommodations

- Religious, medical, or disability-related dress accommodations: Contact HR
- Temperature-related accommodations: Layers are encouraged; contact Facilities for workspace adjustments

### Enforcement

- Managers address dress code concerns privately and respectfully
- First occurrence: Verbal reminder
- Repeated issues: Written counseling
- Focus is on maintaining a professional, inclusive environment
Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Dress code and appearance policy — Administration department
Content:
## Question

What is the dress code policy for Administration employees?

## Answer

The company maintains a business casual dress code with flexibility for different work environments.

### Dress Code by Setting

**Office (Business Casual)**
- Collared shirts, blouses, sweaters, cardigans
- Slacks, chinos, skirts, dresses
- Clean, closed-toe shoes (sneakers acceptable if clean and professional)
- Jeans allowed on Fridays and during designated casual periods

**Client-Facing / External Meetings (Business Professional)**
- Suits, blazers, professional dresses
- Dress shoes
- Conservative accessories
- Your manager will advise when business professional is expected

**Casual Fridays / Company Events**

Author: Learning & Development

Title: Data privacy and employee records — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What are the company's policies on employee data privacy and access to personnel records for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company is committed to protecting employee personal information.

### What's in Your Personnel File

- Employment application and resume
- Offer letter and employment agreement
- Performance reviews and goal documents
- Disciplinary records
- Compensation history
- Training records and certifications
- Benefits enrollment forms
- Emergency contact information

### Stored Separately (Not in Personnel File)

- Medical records (ADA accommodations, FMLA, drug test results)
- I-9 forms
- Background check results
- EEO self-identification data
- EAP records (maintained by EAP provider, not accessible by the company)

### Your Rights

**Access**: You have the right to review your personnel file
1. Submit a request to HR at hr-records@company.com
2. HR arranges a supervised review within 5 business days
3. You may request copies of documents in your file
4. You may submit a written rebuttal to any document you disagree with

**Correction**: Request correction of factual errors in your file
- Submit a correction request with supporting documentation
- HR reviews and updates within 10 business days

### Who Can Access Your Records

| Who | What They Can Access |
|-----|---------------------|
| You | Your complete personnel file |
| Your Manager | Performance reviews, job description, compensation (current) |
| HR | Full access for legitimate business purposes |
| Legal | When required for legal proceedings |
| Payroll | Compensation, tax, and banking information |
| External (reference checks) | Dates of employment and title only (unless you authorize more) |

### Data Protection

- Employee records stored in encrypted HRIS system
- Access logged and audited quarterly
- Paper files stored in locked cabinets in HR
- Records retained per legal requirements (typically 7 years post-separation)
- Destruction: Shredded (paper) or securely deleted (electronic)

### Reporting Concerns

- Suspected privacy breach: Report to privacy@company.com or the Ethics Hotline
- HR investigates all reported breaches within 48 hours

1. Submit a request to HR at hr-records@company.com
2. HR arranges a supervised review within 5 business days
3. You may request copies of documents in your file
4. You may submit a written rebuttal to any document you disagree with

**Correction**: Request correction of factual errors in your file
- Submit a correction request with supporting documentation
- HR reviews and updates within 10 business days

### Who Can Access Your Records

| Who | What They Can Access |
|-----|---------------------|
| You | Your complete personnel file |
| Your Manager | Performance reviews, job description, compensation (current) |
| HR | Full access for legitimate business purposes |
| Legal | When required for legal proceedings |
| Payroll | Compensation, tax, and banking information |
| External (reference checks) | Dates of employment and title only (unless you authorize more) |

### Data Protection

- Employee records stored in encrypted HRIS system
- Access logged and audited quarterly
- Paper files stored in locked cabinets in HR
- Records retained per legal requirements (typically 7 years post-separation)
- Destruction: Shredded (paper) or securely deleted (electronic)

### Reporting Concerns

- Suspected privacy breach: Report to privacy@company.com or the Ethics Hotline
- HR investigates all reported breaches within 48 hours
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: Data privacy and employee records — Administration department
Content:
## Question

What are the company's policies on employee data privacy and access to personnel records for Administration employees?

## Answer

The company is committed to protecting employee personal information.

### What's in Your Personnel File

- Employment application and resume
- Offer letter and employment agreement
- Performance reviews and goal documents
- Disciplinary records
- Compensation history
- Training records and certifications
- Benefits enrollment forms
- Emergency contact information

### Stored Separately (Not in Personnel File)

- I-9 forms
- Background check results

- EAP records (maintained by EAP provider, not accessible by the company)

Author: HR Generalist

Title: Intellectual property and invention assignment — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What are the intellectual property policies for Engineering employees?

## Answer

All employees must understand and comply with the company's intellectual property (IP) policies.

### What the Company Owns

Per the IP Assignment Agreement (signed at hire), the company owns:
- Inventions, discoveries, and innovations made during work hours or using company resources
- Software, code, designs, and creative works produced in the scope of employment
- Trade secrets and confidential information developed for the company
- Patents, copyrights, and trademarks arising from company work

### What You Retain

- Inventions created entirely on your own time, without company resources, and unrelated to company business
- Pre-existing IP disclosed on your Prior Inventions Disclosure (Exhibit A of your employment agreement)
- Personal creative works unrelated to your job

### Invention Disclosure

If you create something that may be patentable or valuable:
1. Complete an Invention Disclosure Form (Legal Portal > Forms > Invention Disclosure)
2. Include: description, contributors, date of conception, potential applications
3. Legal reviews within 30 days
4. Company decides whether to pursue patent protection
5. Inventors are recognized and may receive a $2,500 patent bonus upon filing

### Open Source Contributions

- Contributing to open source projects requires approval from Legal and your manager
- Submit an Open Source Contribution Request via the Legal Portal
- Approval considers: license compatibility, competitive impact, and IP risk
- Many contributions are approved — the process ensures proper review

### Side Projects

- Permitted if they don't conflict with your role or use company resources
- Disclose any side projects that are in the same industry or field as your work
- Use the Side Project Disclosure form on the Legal Portal
- When in doubt, disclose — it protects both you and the company

### Confidentiality Obligations

- NDA obligations survive employment (typically 2 years, or indefinitely for trade secrets)
- Do not share proprietary information with new employers
- Return all company materials and delete company data upon separation
- Report any suspected IP theft to Legal immediately
Author: HR Generalist

- Trade secrets and confidential information developed for the company
- Patents, copyrights, and trademarks arising from company work

### What You Retain

- Inventions created entirely on your own time, without company resources, and unrelated to company business
- Pre-existing IP disclosed on your Prior Inventions Disclosure (Exhibit A of your employment agreement)
- Personal creative works unrelated to your job

### Invention Disclosure

If you create something that may be patentable or valuable:
1. Complete an Invention Disclosure Form (Legal Portal > Forms > Invention Disclosure)
2. Include: description, contributors, date of conception, potential applications
3. Legal reviews within 30 days
4. Company decides whether to pursue patent protection
5. Inventors are recognized and may receive a $2,500 patent bonus upon filing

### Open Source Contributions

- Contributing to open source projects requires approval from Legal and your manager
- Submit an Open Source Contribution Request via the Legal Portal
- Approval considers: license compatibility, competitive impact, and IP risk
- Many contributions are approved — the process ensures proper review

### Side Projects

- Permitted if they don't conflict with your role or use company resources
- Disclose any side projects that are in the same industry or field as your work
- Use the Side Project Disclosure form on the Legal Portal
- When in doubt, disclose — it protects both you and the company

### Confidentiality Obligations

- NDA obligations survive employment (typically 2 years, or indefinitely for trade secrets)
- Do not share proprietary information with new employers
- Return all company materials and delete company data upon separation
- Report any suspected IP theft to Legal immediately
Author: HR Benefits Team

Title: Bonus and incentive pay structure — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does the bonus and incentive pay structure work for Engineering employees?

## Answer

The company offers several variable compensation programs in addition to base salary.

### Annual Performance Bonus

| Level | Target Bonus (% of Base) | Range |
|-------|------------------------|-------|
| L1-L2 (Individual Contributor) | 5% | 0-10% |
| L3-L4 (Senior IC / Lead) | 10% | 0-20% |
| L5 (Manager) | 15% | 0-30% |
| L6 (Director) | 20% | 0-40% |
| L7+ (VP and above) | 25-40% | 0-80% |

### Bonus Calculation

Your actual bonus depends on three factors:
1. **Company Performance** (40% weight): Revenue and profit vs. annual targets
2. **Team Performance** (30% weight): Department/team OKR achievement
3. **Individual Performance** (30% weight): Your performance rating

Formula: `Base Salary × Target % × (Company × 0.4 + Team × 0.3 + Individual × 0.3)`

### Bonus Timeline

| Month | Activity |
|-------|----------|
| January | Prior year financials finalized |
| February | Company and team multipliers calculated |
| March | Individual multipliers set during performance reviews |
| March 15 | Bonus payouts processed |
| March 31 | Bonuses deposited (separate from regular paycheck) |

### Other Incentive Programs

**Spot Bonuses**
- Manager-discretionary awards of $250-$2,500
- For exceptional contributions, above-and-beyond effort, or project milestones
- Can be nominated by anyone, approved by manager + HR
- Paid on the next payroll cycle

**Sales Commission** (Sales team only)
- Detailed in individual sales compensation plan
- Paid monthly based on closed deals
- Accelerators for over-quota performance

**Project Bonuses**
- For critical projects with defined milestones
- Approved by VP+ in advance
- Typically $1,000-$10,000 depending on project scope

### Tax Treatment

- All bonuses are taxable income
- Federal supplemental withholding rate: 22% (or 37% for amounts over $1M)
- State taxes apply
- Bonuses do not count toward 401(k) matching calculations

### Tax Treatment

- All bonuses are taxable income
- Federal supplemental withholding rate: 22% (or 37% for amounts over $1M)
- State taxes apply
- Bonuses do not count toward 401(k) matching calculations
Author: HR Compliance Team

Title: Bonus and incentive pay structure — Quality Assurance department
Content:
## Question

How does the bonus and incentive pay structure work for Quality Assurance employees?

## Answer

The company offers several variable compensation programs in addition to base salary.

### Annual Performance Bonus

| Level | Target Bonus (% of Base) | Range |
|-------|------------------------|-------|
| L1-L2 (Individual Contributor) | 5% | 0-10% |
| L3-L4 (Senior IC / Lead) | 10% | 0-20% |
| L5 (Manager) | 15% | 0-30% |
| L6 (Director) | 20% | 0-40% |
| L7+ (VP and above) | 25-40% | 0-80% |

### Bonus Calculation

Your actual bonus depends on three factors:
1. **Company Performance** (40% weight): Revenue and profit vs. annual targets
2. **Team Performance** (30% weight): Department/team OKR achievement
3. **Individual Performance** (30% weight): Your performance rating

Formula: `Base Salary × Target % × (Company × 0.4 + Team × 0.3 + Individual × 0.3)`

### Bonus Timeline

| Month | Activity |
|-------|----------|
| January | Prior year financials finalized |
| February | Company and team multipliers calculated |
| March | Individual multipliers set during performance reviews |
| March 15 | Bonus payouts processed |
| March 31 | Bonuses deposited (separate from regular paycheck) |

### Other Incentive Programs

**Spot Bonuses**
- Manager-discretionary awards of $250-$2,500
- For exceptional contributions, above-and-beyond effort, or project milestones
- Can be nominated by anyone, approved by manager + HR
- Paid on the next payroll cycle

**Sales Commission** (Sales team only)
- Detailed in individual sales compensation plan
- Paid monthly based on closed deals
- Accelerators for over-quota performance

**Project Bonuses**
- For critical projects with defined milestones
- Approved by VP+ in advance
- Typically $1,000-$10,000 depending on project scope

### Tax Treatment

- All bonuses are taxable income
- Federal supplemental withholding rate: 22% (or 37% for amounts over $1M)
- State taxes apply
- Bonuses do not count toward 401(k) matching calculations

Title: How to request a pay advance — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

Can a Engineering employee request a pay advance in an emergency?

## Answer

The company offers emergency pay advances for employees facing financial hardship.

### Eligibility

- Full-time employees who have completed 90 days of employment
- Maximum one pay advance per 12-month period
- Must have sufficient earned but unpaid wages to cover the advance

### Advance Limits

- Maximum: 50% of one pay period's net pay
- Minimum: $200
- Cannot exceed wages already earned in the current pay period

### How to Request

1. Contact the Payroll Team at payroll@company.com or ext. 4100
2. Complete the Pay Advance Request Form (HR Portal > Forms > Pay Advance)
3. Include the reason for the request (documentation may be required for amounts over $500)
4. Manager notification (not approval) is required
5. HR/Payroll reviews and processes within 2 business days

### Repayment

- Automatically deducted from your next paycheck
- For larger advances, a 2-paycheck repayment plan may be arranged
- Repayment agreement signed before advance is issued
- No interest charged

### Alternatives to Pay Advances

| Resource | Details |
|----------|---------|
| EAP Financial Counseling | Free budgeting and debt management help (800-555-0123) |
| Emergency Assistance Fund | Grants of up to $2,500 for qualified hardships (see HR Portal) |
| 401(k) Loan | Borrow up to 50% of vested balance (up to $50,000) |
| Credit Union | Company credit union offers low-interest emergency loans |
| United Way 211 | Community assistance resources (dial 211) |

### Emergency Assistance Fund

For more serious financial hardships (natural disaster, medical emergency, death in family):
- Grants of $500-$2,500 (not a loan — does not need to be repaid)
- Funded by employee donations and company matching
- Apply via HR Portal > Emergency Assistance
- Reviewed by a committee within 5 business days
- Confidential — your manager is not informed
Author: Learning & Development

1. Contact the Payroll Team at payroll@company.com or ext. 4100
2. Complete the Pay Advance Request Form (HR Portal > Forms > Pay Advance)
3. Include the reason for the request (documentation may be required for amounts over $500)
4. Manager notification (not approval) is required
5. HR/Payroll reviews and processes within 2 business days

### Repayment

- Automatically deducted from your next paycheck
- For larger advances, a 2-paycheck repayment plan may be arranged
- Repayment agreement signed before advance is issued
- No interest charged

### Alternatives to Pay Advances

| Resource | Details |
|----------|---------|
| EAP Financial Counseling | Free budgeting and debt management help (800-555-0123) |
| Emergency Assistance Fund | Grants of up to $2,500 for qualified hardships (see HR Portal) |
| 401(k) Loan | Borrow up to 50% of vested balance (up to $50,000) |
| Credit Union | Company credit union offers low-interest emergency loans |
| United Way 211 | Community assistance resources (dial 211) |

### Emergency Assistance Fund

For more serious financial hardships (natural disaster, medical emergency, death in family):
- Grants of $500-$2,500 (not a loan — does not need to be repaid)
- Funded by employee donations and company matching
- Apply via HR Portal > Emergency Assistance
- Reviewed by a committee within 5 business days
- Confidential — your manager is not informed
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: How to request a pay advance — Administration department
Content:
## Question

Can a Administration employee request a pay advance in an emergency?

## Answer

The company offers emergency pay advances for employees facing financial hardship.

### Eligibility

- Full-time employees who have completed 90 days of employment
- Maximum one pay advance per 12-month period
- Must have sufficient earned but unpaid wages to cover the advance

### Advance Limits

- Maximum: 50% of one pay period's net pay
- Minimum: $200
- Cannot exceed wages already earned in the current pay period

### How to Request

1. Contact the Payroll Team at payroll@company.com or ext. 4100
2. Complete the Pay Advance Request Form (HR Portal > Forms > Pay Advance)
3. Include the reason for the request (documentation may be required for amounts over $500)
4. Manager notification (not approval) is required
5. HR/Payroll reviews and processes within 2 business days

### Repayment

- Automatically deducted from your next paycheck
- For larger advances, a 2-paycheck repayment plan may be arranged
