Title: Year-end tax documents and W-2 — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

When and how do Engineering employees receive year-end tax documents?

## Answer

### W-2 Distribution

- **Deadline**: W-2s are available by January 31 each year
- **Electronic**: Available on ess.company.com > Pay > Tax Documents
  - Opt-in to electronic delivery during onboarding or via the portal
  - Available as soon as January 15 (electronic)
- **Paper**: Mailed to your home address on file by January 31
  - Uses the address on file as of December 31

### W-2 Contents

| Box | Description |
|-----|-------------|
| Box 1 | Wages, tips, other compensation (taxable income) |
| Box 2 | Federal income tax withheld |
| Box 3 | Social Security wages |
| Box 4 | Social Security tax withheld |
| Box 5 | Medicare wages |
| Box 6 | Medicare tax withheld |
| Box 12 | Codes for 401(k) contributions (D), HSA (W), dependent care (10), etc. |
| Box 14 | Other items (state disability, union dues, etc.) |

### Common Questions

**"My W-2 wages don't match my salary"**
- Pre-tax deductions (401k, medical, HSA, FSA, transit) reduce Box 1
- Example: $80,000 salary - $8,000 401k - $3,000 medical - $2,000 HSA = $67,000 in Box 1

**"I need a corrected W-2"**
- Contact Payroll at payroll@company.com
- Corrected W-2c issued within 10 business days
- Common reasons: wrong SSN, incorrect address, incorrect withholding

**"I didn't receive my W-2"**
- Check the portal first (electronic delivery)
- If not available by February 15, contact Payroll
- Verify your mailing address is correct
- A replacement can be mailed or emailed

### Other Tax Documents

| Document | Who Receives | When |
|----------|-------------|------|
| 1095-C (ACA) | All benefits-eligible employees | By March 2 |
| 1099-R | Employees who took 401(k) distributions | By January 31 |
| 1099-NEC | Independent contractors | By January 31 |

### Tax Filing Resources

- Free tax preparation assistance through EAP (for simple returns)
- TurboTax/H&R Block discount through employee perks portal
- Company does not provide individual tax advice — consult a tax professional
Author: Payroll Team

- Example: $80,000 salary - $8,000 401k - $3,000 medical - $2,000 HSA = $67,000 in Box 1

**"I need a corrected W-2"**
- Contact Payroll at payroll@company.com
- Corrected W-2c issued within 10 business days
- Common reasons: wrong SSN, incorrect address, incorrect withholding

**"I didn't receive my W-2"**
- Check the portal first (electronic delivery)
- If not available by February 15, contact Payroll
- Verify your mailing address is correct
- A replacement can be mailed or emailed

### Other Tax Documents

| Document | Who Receives | When |
|----------|-------------|------|
| 1095-C (ACA) | All benefits-eligible employees | By March 2 |
| 1099-R | Employees who took 401(k) distributions | By January 31 |
| 1099-NEC | Independent contractors | By January 31 |

### Tax Filing Resources

- Free tax preparation assistance through EAP (for simple returns)
- TurboTax/H&R Block discount through employee perks portal
- Company does not provide individual tax advice — consult a tax professional

Title: LinkedIn Learning access — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How do Engineering employees access LinkedIn Learning?

## Answer

All employees have free, unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning's library of 16,000+ courses.

### Getting Started

1. Go to learn.company.com > "LinkedIn Learning" (or directly to linkedin.com/learning)
2. Sign in with your company email (SSO enabled)
3. First-time users: Link your LinkedIn profile (optional but recommended)
4. Start browsing and learning!

### What's Available

- **Business**: Leadership, management, communication, project management, strategy
- **Technology**: Programming, cloud computing, data science, cybersecurity, IT
- **Creative**: Design, video, photography, UX, web development

### Features

- **Learning Paths**: Curated sequences of courses for specific skills or roles
- **Certificates**: Completion certificates for each course (shareable on LinkedIn)
- **Offline Access**: Download courses on the mobile app for offline viewing
- **Bookmarks**: Save courses to "My Learning" for later
- **Notes**: Take notes within courses (synced across devices)

### Recommended Learning Paths by Role

| Role Type | Suggested Path | Duration |
|-----------|---------------|----------|
| New Manager | Becoming a Manager | 12 hours |
| Project Lead | Project Management Foundations | 8 hours |
| Data Analyst | Data Analytics Foundations | 15 hours |
| Developer | Software Development Best Practices | 10 hours |
| All Employees | Communication Foundations | 6 hours |

### Tracking and Reporting

- Your manager can see courses you've completed (if assigned)
- Self-directed learning is private unless you choose to share
- Learning hours count toward your development goals
- Include completed courses in your performance self-assessment

### Manager Features

- Assign specific courses or learning paths to your team
- Track team learning progress on the manager dashboard
- Recommend content to team members
- Create custom learning paths for your team

### Mobile App

- Available for iOS and Android
- Search "LinkedIn Learning" in the app store
- Sign in with your company email
- Download courses for offline viewing during commutes or travel

Title: LinkedIn Learning access — Logistics department
Content:
## Question

How do Logistics employees access LinkedIn Learning?

## Answer

All employees have free, unlimited access to LinkedIn Learning's library of 16,000+ courses.

### Getting Started

1. Go to learn.company.com > "LinkedIn Learning" (or directly to linkedin.com/learning)
2. Sign in with your company email (SSO enabled)
3. First-time users: Link your LinkedIn profile (optional but recommended)
4. Start browsing and learning!

### What's Available

- **Business**: Leadership, management, communication, project management, strategy
- **Technology**: Programming, cloud computing, data science, cybersecurity, IT
- **Creative**: Design, video, photography, UX, web development

### Features

- **Learning Paths**: Curated sequences of courses for specific skills or roles
- **Certificates**: Completion certificates for each course (shareable on LinkedIn)
- **Offline Access**: Download courses on the mobile app for offline viewing
- **Bookmarks**: Save courses to "My Learning" for later
- **Notes**: Take notes within courses (synced across devices)

### Recommended Learning Paths by Role

| Role Type | Suggested Path | Duration |
|-----------|---------------|----------|
| New Manager | Becoming a Manager | 12 hours |
| Project Lead | Project Management Foundations | 8 hours |
| Data Analyst | Data Analytics Foundations | 15 hours |
| Developer | Software Development Best Practices | 10 hours |
| All Employees | Communication Foundations | 6 hours |

### Tracking and Reporting

- Your manager can see courses you've completed (if assigned)
- Self-directed learning is private unless you choose to share
- Learning hours count toward your development goals
- Include completed courses in your performance self-assessment

### Manager Features

- Assign specific courses or learning paths to your team
- Track team learning progress on the manager dashboard
- Recommend content to team members
- Create custom learning paths for your team

### Mobile App

- Available for iOS and Android
- Search "LinkedIn Learning" in the app store
- Sign in with your company email
- Download courses for offline viewing during commutes or travel
Author: HR Generalist

Title: Conference and event attendance policy — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How can Engineering employees attend professional conferences or industry events?

## Answer

The company supports professional development through conference and event attendance.

### Allowance

| Level | Annual Conference Budget | Events per Year |
|-------|------------------------|-----------------|
| L1-L2 | $1,500 | 1 |
| L3-L4 | $3,000 | 2 |
| L5-L6 | $5,000 | 2-3 |
| L7+ | $8,000 | As needed |

Budget covers: registration, travel, hotel, and meals per the travel policy.

### Approval Process

1. Identify the conference/event and how it supports your development goals
2. Submit a Conference Attendance Request via the HR Portal
   - Include: event name, dates, location, cost estimate, business justification
3. Manager reviews and approves (within 5 business days)
4. For costs over $3,000: Director approval also required
5. Once approved, book travel per the company travel policy

### What's Covered

- Conference registration fees
- Airfare (economy class, booked 14+ days in advance)
- Hotel (up to company rate or $250/night)
- Ground transportation (rideshare, taxi, rental car per policy)
- Meals (up to $75/day)
- Reasonable Wi-Fi or business center charges

### Speaking Engagements

- Employees are encouraged to speak at conferences
- Speaker engagements have a separate, higher budget ($8,000)
- Speaking proposals must be reviewed by Marketing/Communications
- Presentation materials must not contain proprietary information
- Submit speaking requests at least 8 weeks before the event

### After the Event

- Submit expenses within 30 days
- Share key takeaways with your team (presentation, email summary, or lunch-and-learn)
- Post relevant insights on internal collaboration channels
- Apply learnings to your work and note in your development goals

### Virtual Conferences

- Approved at manager discretion (no formal request needed for free events)
- Paid virtual events follow the same approval process
- Block your calendar and treat it as focused learning time
Author: HR Benefits Team

How can Logistics employees attend professional conferences or industry events?

## Answer

The company supports professional development through conference and event attendance.

### Allowance

| Level | Annual Conference Budget | Events per Year |
|-------|------------------------|-----------------|
| L1-L2 | $1,500 | 1 |
| L3-L4 | $3,000 | 2 |
| L5-L6 | $5,000 | 2-3 |
| L7+ | $8,000 | As needed |

Budget covers: registration, travel, hotel, and meals per the travel policy.

### Approval Process

1. Identify the conference/event and how it supports your development goals
2. Submit a Conference Attendance Request via the HR Portal
   - Include: event name, dates, location, cost estimate, business justification
3. Manager reviews and approves (within 5 business days)
4. For costs over $3,000: Director approval also required
5. Once approved, book travel per the company travel policy

### What's Covered

- Conference registration fees
- Airfare (economy class, booked 14+ days in advance)
- Hotel (up to company rate or $250/night)
- Ground transportation (rideshare, taxi, rental car per policy)
- Meals (up to $75/day)
- Reasonable Wi-Fi or business center charges

### Speaking Engagements

- Employees are encouraged to speak at conferences
- Speaker engagements have a separate, higher budget ($8,000)
- Speaking proposals must be reviewed by Marketing/Communications
- Presentation materials must not contain proprietary information
- Submit speaking requests at least 8 weeks before the event

### After the Event

- Submit expenses within 30 days
- Share key takeaways with your team (presentation, email summary, or lunch-and-learn)
- Post relevant insights on internal collaboration channels
- Apply learnings to your work and note in your development goals

### Virtual Conferences

- Approved at manager discretion (no formal request needed for free events)
- Paid virtual events follow the same approval process
- Block your calendar and treat it as focused learning time
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: First aid and CPR training — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

Is first aid and CPR training available for Engineering employees?

## Answer

Yes! The company offers free first aid, CPR, and AED certification training.

### Training Options

| Course | Duration | Certification Valid |
|--------|----------|-------------------|
| Adult First Aid/CPR/AED | 4 hours | 2 years |
| Pediatric First Aid | 2 hours (add-on) | 2 years |
| Bloodborne Pathogens | 1 hour | 1 year |
| Stop the Bleed | 2 hours | No expiration |

### Schedule

- Classes offered monthly (check the LMS for dates)
- On-site training in the main conference room
- Certified American Red Cross instructors
- Maximum 20 participants per class

### How to Enroll

1. Log in to learn.company.com
2. Go to "Safety Training" > "First Aid/CPR/AED"
3. Select an available date
4. Training counts as work time (no PTO needed)
5. Certification card issued upon successful completion

### Who Should Take This Training

**Required for:**
- Floor Wardens and safety committee members
- Employees in safety-sensitive roles
- Managers with 10+ direct reports

**Strongly encouraged for:**
- All employees (it can save lives!)
- Parents and caregivers
- Employees who work with the public

### First Aid Kit Locations

| Location | Kit Type |
|----------|---------|
| Each floor break room | Standard workplace kit |
| Loading dock | Industrial first aid kit |
| Fitness center | Sports first aid kit |
| Each floor reception | Standard workplace kit |
| Company vehicles | Vehicle first aid kit |

### AED Locations

- Main lobby (left of reception)
- Each floor near elevators
- Fitness center
- Loading dock office
- All AEDs checked monthly by Facilities

### In Case of Emergency

1. Call 911
2. Begin CPR if needed (push hard, push fast — 100-120 compressions/minute)
3. Send someone to get the nearest AED
4. Follow AED voice prompts
5. Continue until EMS arrives
Author: HR Operations

Content:
## Question

Is first aid and CPR training available for Logistics employees?

## Answer

Yes! The company offers free first aid, CPR, and AED certification training.

### Training Options

| Course | Duration | Certification Valid |
|--------|----------|-------------------|
| Adult First Aid/CPR/AED | 4 hours | 2 years |
| Pediatric First Aid | 2 hours (add-on) | 2 years |
| Bloodborne Pathogens | 1 hour | 1 year |
| Stop the Bleed | 2 hours | No expiration |

### Schedule

- Classes offered monthly (check the LMS for dates)
- On-site training in the main conference room
- Certified American Red Cross instructors
- Maximum 20 participants per class

### How to Enroll

1. Log in to learn.company.com
2. Go to "Safety Training" > "First Aid/CPR/AED"
3. Select an available date
4. Training counts as work time (no PTO needed)
5. Certification card issued upon successful completion

### Who Should Take This Training

**Required for:**
- Floor Wardens and safety committee members
- Employees in safety-sensitive roles
- Managers with 10+ direct reports

**Strongly encouraged for:**
- All employees (it can save lives!)
- Parents and caregivers
- Employees who work with the public

### First Aid Kit Locations

| Location | Kit Type |
|----------|---------|
| Each floor break room | Standard workplace kit |
| Loading dock | Industrial first aid kit |
| Fitness center | Sports first aid kit |
| Each floor reception | Standard workplace kit |
| Company vehicles | Vehicle first aid kit |

### AED Locations

- Main lobby (left of reception)
- Each floor near elevators
- Fitness center
- Loading dock office
- All AEDs checked monthly by Facilities

### In Case of Emergency

1. Call 911
2. Begin CPR if needed (push hard, push fast — 100-120 compressions/minute)
3. Send someone to get the nearest AED
4. Follow AED voice prompts
5. Continue until EMS arrives
Author: HR Generalist

Title: Reporting safety hazards — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How should Engineering employees report workplace safety hazards?

## Answer

Every employee has the right and responsibility to report safety hazards. You will never face retaliation for reporting a safety concern.

### What to Report

- Wet floors, spills, or tripping hazards
- Broken equipment or furniture
- Exposed wiring or electrical hazards
- Blocked emergency exits or fire equipment
- Chemical spills or fumes
- Inadequate lighting
- Temperature extremes
- Ergonomic concerns
- Suspicious packages or persons
- Any condition that could cause injury

### How to Report

**Immediate Danger:**
1. Remove yourself and others from the area
2. Call Security at ext. 5100
3. Call 911 if life-threatening
4. Do not attempt to fix dangerous situations yourself

**Non-Emergency Hazards:**

| Method | When to Use |
|--------|------------|
| Safety Hotline (ext. 5600) | Urgent but not life-threatening |
| Online form (HR Portal > Safety > Report Hazard) | Standard reporting |
| Email: safety@company.com | Detailed reports with photos |
| Tell your manager | Quick verbal report |
| Anonymous hotline (800-555-0789) | If you prefer anonymity |

### What Happens After You Report

1. **Acknowledgment**: EHS acknowledges receipt within 4 hours (business hours)
2. **Assessment**: Hazard assessed and prioritized
   - Critical: Addressed within 24 hours
   - High: Addressed within 3 business days
   - Medium: Addressed within 10 business days
   - Low: Scheduled for next maintenance cycle
3. **Correction**: Hazard corrected or mitigated
4. **Follow-up**: Reporter notified of resolution
5. **Documentation**: Logged in safety management system for trend analysis

### Safety Committee

- Meets monthly to review hazard reports and safety metrics
- Includes representatives from each department
- Open to employee observers
- Meeting minutes posted on the HR Portal > Safety
- Interested in joining? Contact safety@company.com

### Your Rights

- Right to report without fear of retaliation
- Right to refuse unsafe work (notify your manager and EHS)
- Right to know about hazards in your workplace (Safety Data Sheets available in each area)
- Right to participate in safety inspections and committees
Author: HR Operations

**Immediate Danger:**
1. Remove yourself and others from the area
2. Call Security at ext. 5100
3. Call 911 if life-threatening
4. Do not attempt to fix dangerous situations yourself

**Non-Emergency Hazards:**

| Method | When to Use |
|--------|------------|
| Safety Hotline (ext. 5600) | Urgent but not life-threatening |
| Online form (HR Portal > Safety > Report Hazard) | Standard reporting |
| Email: safety@company.com | Detailed reports with photos |
| Tell your manager | Quick verbal report |
| Anonymous hotline (800-555-0789) | If you prefer anonymity |

### What Happens After You Report

1. **Acknowledgment**: EHS acknowledges receipt within 4 hours (business hours)
2. **Assessment**: Hazard assessed and prioritized
   - Critical: Addressed within 24 hours
   - High: Addressed within 3 business days
   - Medium: Addressed within 10 business days
   - Low: Scheduled for next maintenance cycle
3. **Correction**: Hazard corrected or mitigated
4. **Follow-up**: Reporter notified of resolution
5. **Documentation**: Logged in safety management system for trend analysis

### Safety Committee

- Meets monthly to review hazard reports and safety metrics
- Includes representatives from each department
- Open to employee observers
- Meeting minutes posted on the HR Portal > Safety
- Interested in joining? Contact safety@company.com

### Your Rights

- Right to report without fear of retaliation
- Right to refuse unsafe work (notify your manager and EHS)
- Right to know about hazards in your workplace (Safety Data Sheets available in each area)
- Right to participate in safety inspections and committees

Title: Interview process for hiring managers — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

What is the standard interview process that Engineering hiring managers should follow?

## Answer

Following a consistent, structured interview process ensures fair evaluation and reduces bias.

### Interview Process Overview

| Stage | Owner | Duration | Purpose |
|-------|-------|----------|---------|
| 1. Recruiter Screen | Talent Acquisition | 30 min phone | Qualifications, salary, availability |
| 2. Hiring Manager Screen | Hiring Manager | 45 min video | Role fit, experience deep-dive |
| 3. Panel Interview | 2-3 team members | 60 min | Technical/functional skills |
| 4. Culture Interview | Cross-functional peer | 30 min | Values alignment, collaboration |
| 5. Final Interview | Skip-level manager | 30 min | Leadership endorsement |

### Structured Interview Guidelines

**Before the Interview:**
- Review the job description and required competencies
- Prepare 5-7 behavioral and situational questions from the approved question bank
- Review the candidate's resume (do not review social media profiles)
- Coordinate with other interviewers to avoid duplicate questions

**During the Interview:**
- Ask the same core questions to every candidate for the role
- Use the STAR method (Situation, Task, Action, Result) for behavioral questions
- Take notes during the interview (use the Interview Scorecard)
- Allow time for the candidate to ask questions
- Do not ask about protected characteristics (age, marital status, religion, disability, pregnancy, etc.)

**After the Interview:**
- Complete the Interview Scorecard within 24 hours
- Rate each competency on a 1-5 scale with supporting notes
- Provide a clear hire/no-hire recommendation with justification
- Submit scorecard via the Recruiting Platform

### Debrief Process

1. All interviewers submit scorecards independently (no discussion before submission)
2. Recruiter schedules a 30-minute debrief within 48 hours of final interview
3. Each interviewer shares their assessment
4. Group discusses and reaches consensus
5. Hiring manager makes final decision
6. Recruiter extends offer or sends rejection

### Prohibited Questions

Never ask about:
- Age or date of birth
- Marital status or family plans
- Pregnancy or childcare arrangements
- Religion or religious practices
- National origin or citizenship (only: "Are you authorized to work in the US?")
- Disability or health conditions
- Arrest record (may ask about convictions per local law)
- Salary history (in states where prohibited)

### Questions? Contact Talent Acquisition at recruiting@company.com
Author: HR Compliance Team

- Do not ask about protected characteristics (age, marital status, religion, disability, pregnancy, etc.)

**After the Interview:**
- Complete the Interview Scorecard within 24 hours
- Rate each competency on a 1-5 scale with supporting notes
- Provide a clear hire/no-hire recommendation with justification
- Submit scorecard via the Recruiting Platform

### Debrief Process

1. All interviewers submit scorecards independently (no discussion before submission)
2. Recruiter schedules a 30-minute debrief within 48 hours of final interview
3. Each interviewer shares their assessment
4. Group discusses and reaches consensus
5. Hiring manager makes final decision
6. Recruiter extends offer or sends rejection

### Prohibited Questions

Never ask about:
- Age or date of birth
- Marital status or family plans
- Pregnancy or childcare arrangements
- Religion or religious practices
- National origin or citizenship (only: "Are you authorized to work in the US?")
- Disability or health conditions
- Arrest record (may ask about convictions per local law)
- Salary history (in states where prohibited)

### Questions? Contact Talent Acquisition at recruiting@company.com

Title: Internship program — Engineering department
Content:
## Question

How does the internship program work in Engineering?

## Answer

The company runs a robust internship program to develop early-career talent and build our future pipeline.

### Program Details

| Feature | Details |
|---------|---------|
| Duration | 10-12 weeks (May/June through August) |
| Hours | Full-time (40 hours/week) |
| Pay | $25-45/hour depending on role and education level |
| Housing Stipend | $3,000 for interns relocating 50+ miles |
| Benefits | Not benefits-eligible; workers' comp covered |

### Intern Experience

**Week 1: Orientation**
- Company overview and culture immersion
- Meet fellow interns (cohort of 15-30)
- Assign manager and mentor (different people)
- Receive project brief and goals

**Weeks 2-10: Project Work**
- Work on a meaningful, scoped project with real business impact
- Weekly 1:1 with manager
- Bi-weekly check-in with mentor
- Participate in team meetings and daily standups
- Attend intern-specific programming:
  - Executive speaker series (weekly)
  - Professional development workshops (bi-weekly)
  - Social events and team-building (bi-weekly)

**Week 11-12: Wrap-Up**
- Present project results to leadership (Intern Showcase)
- Complete self-assessment and receive manager feedback
- Exit interview with HR
- Return-offer decision communicated within 2 weeks of program end

### Conversion to Full-Time

- Top-performing interns receive return offers for post-graduation full-time positions
- Historical conversion rate: ~60%
- Return offer includes: starting salary, sign-on bonus, start date flexibility
- Offers valid for 12 months

### For Hiring Managers: Requesting an Intern

1. Submit an Intern Request via the Recruiting Platform by January 15
2. Include: project description, skills needed, team, and manager
3. Talent Acquisition reviews and approves
4. Recruiting begins in February at target universities
5. Offers extended by March 31
6. Manager completes Intern Manager Training (2 hours, required)

### Internship Program Contact

- Program Manager: internships@company.com
- Talent Acquisition: recruiting@company.com
Author: Employee Relations Team

Title: Internship program — Product department
Content:
## Question

How does the internship program work in Product?

## Answer

The company runs a robust internship program to develop early-career talent and build our future pipeline.

### Program Details

| Feature | Details |
|---------|---------|
| Duration | 10-12 weeks (May/June through August) |
| Hours | Full-time (40 hours/week) |
| Pay | $25-45/hour depending on role and education level |
| Housing Stipend | $3,000 for interns relocating 50+ miles |
| Benefits | Not benefits-eligible; workers' comp covered |

### Intern Experience

**Week 1: Orientation**
- Company overview and culture immersion
- Meet fellow interns (cohort of 15-30)
- Assign manager and mentor (different people)
- Receive project brief and goals

**Weeks 2-10: Project Work**
- Work on a meaningful, scoped project with real business impact
- Weekly 1:1 with manager
- Bi-weekly check-in with mentor
- Participate in team meetings and daily standups
- Attend intern-specific programming:
  - Executive speaker series (weekly)
  - Professional development workshops (bi-weekly)
  - Social events and team-building (bi-weekly)

**Week 11-12: Wrap-Up**
- Present project results to leadership (Intern Showcase)
- Complete self-assessment and receive manager feedback
- Exit interview with HR
- Return-offer decision communicated within 2 weeks of program end

### Conversion to Full-Time

- Top-performing interns receive return offers for post-graduation full-time positions
- Historical conversion rate: ~60%
- Return offer includes: starting salary, sign-on bonus, start date flexibility
- Offers valid for 12 months

### For Hiring Managers: Requesting an Intern

1. Submit an Intern Request via the Recruiting Platform by January 15
2. Include: project description, skills needed, team, and manager
3. Talent Acquisition reviews and approves
4. Recruiting begins in February at target universities
5. Offers extended by March 31
6. Manager completes Intern Manager Training (2 hours, required)

### Internship Program Contact

- Program Manager: internships@company.com
- Talent Acquisition: recruiting@company.com
Author: Employee Relations Team
